unit: Ὅ6 Unit 6: Systems of Linear Equations and Inequalities
topic: Solving Systems Using Elimination and Substitution

questions.question: In ancient Egypt, $2x + 3y = 12$ and $x - 2y = -3$ represent the cost of building two types of temples. What method can be used to solve for $x$ and $y$?
The ancient Greeks used $x + 4y = 10$ and $3x - 2y = 5$ to plan the construction of a theater. What is the value of $y$ when solved using substitution?
The Rosetta Stone has an inscription with $2x - 5y = 11$ and $x + 2y = 5$. How many solutions does this system have?
The ancient Chinese used $3x + 2y = 12$ and $x - 3y = -4$ to design a new type of pagoda. What is the value of $x$ when solved using elimination?
The ancient Mayans used $x + 2y = 7$ and $4x - 3y = 10$ to plan a new trade route. What method is more efficient to solve this system?
The Terracotta Army was built using $2x - 3y = 8$ and $x + 4y = 12$. How many solutions does this system have?
The Great Pyramid of Giza has a system of equations $3x + 2y = 15$ and $2x - 5y = -2$. What is the value of $x$ when solved using substitution?
The Hanging Gardens of Babylon used $x - 2y = 3$ and $4x + 3y = 14$ to design a new irrigation system. What method can be used to solve for $x$ and $y$?
The ancient city of Pompeii has a system of equations $2x + 5y = 20$ and $3x - 2y = 5$. Solve for $x$ and $y$ using elimination and show your work.
The Colosseum in Rome has a system of equations $x + 3y = 12$ and $4x - 2y = 8$. Solve for $x$ and $y$ using substitution and show your work.

teacher_rules: Check for units and dimensions in the answer
Ensure all work is shown for free response questions
Use a calculator only when necessary
